ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 (ScarCruft) ROKRAT C2 via pCloud/Yandex/Dropbox cloud-API abuse
ucs.description: Hunts ROKRAT and CHINOTTO/GOLDBACKDOOR family C2 traffic, which is APT37's signature TTP — bidirectional command and control over legitimate cloud-storage APIs (pCloud, Yandex Disk, Dropbox). Detection fires when a non-browser/non-O365 process talks to these specific cloud-API hostnames, which is highly anomalous outside of known sync clients.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.url) as url from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.url IN ("*api.pcloud.com*","*eapi.pcloud.com*","*cloud-api.yandex.net*","*content.dropboxapi.com*","*api.dropboxapi.com*","*api.box.com*") OR All_Traffic.dest IN ("api.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com")) AND All_Traffic.app!="chrome" AND All_Traffic.app!="msedge" AND All_Traffic.app!="firefox" AND All_Traffic.app!="Dropbox" AND All_Traffic.app!="Yandex.Disk" AND All_Traffic.app!="pcloud" by All_Traffic.src host All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | where count >= 3
ucs.kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft ROKRAT C2 — non-browser process beaconing to pCloud/Yandex/Dropbox APIs
let _cloud_c2_hosts = dynamic(["api.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","api.box.com"]);
let _legit_clients = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","dropbox.exe","yandexdisk.exe","yandexdisk2.exe","pcloud.exe","box.exe","box drive.exe","onedrive.exe","backupandsync.exe","googledrivefs.exe","outlook.exe","msteams.exe","teams.exe"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (_cloud_c2_hosts)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_legit_clients)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| summarize Connections = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1m)),
            SampleUrls = make_set(RemoteUrl, 5),
            SampleCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessAccountName, RemoteIP
| where Connections >= 3
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft ROKRAT C2 over legitimate cloud-storage APIs
let _cloud_c2_hosts = dynamic(["api.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","api.box.com"]);
let _legit_clients = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","dropbox.exe","yandexdisk.exe","yandexdisk2.exe","pcloud.exe","box.exe","onedrive.exe","googledrivefs.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","msteams.exe"]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where isnotempty(Url) or isnotempty(DstHostname)
| extend HostMatch = tolower(coalesce(DstHostname, Url))
| where HostMatch has_any (_cloud_c2_hosts)
| extend ProcName = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ProcName !in~ (_legit_clients)
| where SrcUsername !endswith "$"
| summarize Connections = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)),
            SampleUrls = make_set(Url, 5),
            SampleCmd = any(ActingProcessCommandLine)
            by DvcHostname, ActingProcessName, SrcUsername, DstIpAddr
| where Connections >= 3
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: ROKRAT — APT37's flagship implant since at least 2017 — and successor families CHINOTTO and GOLDBACKDOOR specifically use pCloud, Yandex Disk, Dropbox and Box REST APIs for both task-fetching and exfil. This is documented in Microsoft TIC (InkySquid), Cisco Talos, ESTsecurity, and Mandiant (TEMP.Reaper) reporting. Filtering to those exact API endpoints AND excluding the legitimate sync clients cat

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 (ScarCruft) LNK-lure execution chain — explorer.exe → cmd/powershell with embedded payload extraction
ucs.description: Hunts the multi-stage LNK initial-access chain APT37 has used in GOLDBACKDOOR, RokRAT-LNK and 2022-2024 campaigns against South Korean / NK-defector targets: a malicious .lnk delivered via spear-phish runs cmd.exe or powershell.exe that reads/extracts a payload from a 'decoy + payload' file in %TEMP% / %APPDATA% (often double-extension or stego-hidden inside an image/HWP).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") AND (Processes.parent_process="*.lnk*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process="*findstr*" OR Processes.process="*Get-Content*" OR Processes.process="*ReadAllBytes*" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*-decode*" OR Processes.process="*certutil*-decode*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft LNK initial-access chain — explorer-spawned interpreter reading a payload
let _interpreters = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe"]);
let _payload_indicators = dynamic([
    "findstr","Get-Content","ReadAllBytes","FromBase64String",
    "-decode","certutil","expand.exe","makecab",
    "AppData\\Local\\Temp","AppData\\Roaming",
    ".lnk",".hwp",".docx",".xlsx",".pdf",
    "-EncodedCommand","-enc ","IEX","Invoke-Expression",
    "DownloadString","WebClient"
]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "explorer.exe"
| where FileName in~ (_interpreters)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_payload_indicators)
   or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has ".lnk"
// APT37 commonly chains: lnk -> cmd /c findstr /V <decoy bytes> X.lnk > %TEMP%\payload.bat (or .ps1 / .vbs)
| extend ChainSig = case(
    ProcessCommandLine has "findstr" and ProcessCommandLine has_any (".lnk",".hwp",".docx",".pdf"), "lnk-stego-extract",
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("FromBase64String","-EncodedCommand","-enc "),                       "powershell-b64",
    ProcessCommandLine has "certutil" and ProcessCommandLine has "-decode",                           "certutil-decode",
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("DownloadString","WebClient","Invoke-WebRequest"),                     "powershell-stager",
    "other")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ChainSig,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT37 / ScarCruft LNK initial-access chain — Sentinel SecurityEvent (4688) variant
let _interpreters = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe"]);
let _payload_indicators = dynamic([
    "findstr","Get-Content","ReadAllBytes","FromBase64String",
    "-decode","certutil","expand.exe",
    "AppData\\Local\\Temp","AppData\\Roaming",
    ".lnk",".hwp",".docx",".xlsx",".pdf",
    "-EncodedCommand","-enc ","IEX","Invoke-Expression",
    "DownloadString","WebClient"
]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName endswith "explorer.exe"
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in~ (_interpreters)
| where CommandLine has_any (_payload_indicators)
| extend ChainSig = case(
    CommandLine has "findstr" and CommandLine has_any (".lnk",".hwp",".docx",".pdf"), "lnk-stego-extract",
    CommandLine has_any ("FromBase64String","-EncodedCommand","-enc "),                "powershell-b64",
    CommandLine has "certutil" and CommandLine has "-decode",                           "certutil-decode",
    CommandLine has_any ("DownloadString","WebClient","Invoke-WebRequest"),              "powershell-stager",
    "other")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ChainSig,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT37 ScarCruft LNK Initial-Access Chain (explorer -> interpreter w/ payload-extraction args)
id: 9c7f4e8a-7a37-4b9e-9c12-apt37lnk0001
status: experimental
description: Detects APT37 / ScarCruft style LNK lure execution where explorer.exe spawns cmd / powershell / mshta / rundll32 / regsvr32 / wscript / cscript with arguments characteristic of payload extraction from a stego-bearing lure file (findstr-on-lnk, certutil -decode, base64 PowerShell, AppData payload paths).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0067/
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/rokrat-malware/
  - https://stairwell.com/news/threat-research-goldbackdoor-apt37/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1027.003
  - attack.g0067
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\explorer.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  selection_args:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'findstr'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - ' -enc '
      - 'certutil'
      - 'IEX'
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'ReadAllBytes'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '.lnk'
      - '.hwp'
  filter_user:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and selection_args and not filter_user
fields:
  - ComputerName
  - User
  - ParentCommandLine
  - CommandLine
  - Image
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate admin scripts launched from desktop shortcuts (rare; usually not in AppData and not chained with findstr/certutil-decode)
  - Software-installer wrappers that use certutil for trusted decoding
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT37/ScarCruft is one of the few mature actors that has consistently — across ROKRAT, GOLDBACKDOOR (Stairwell, 2022) and 2023-2024 ROKRAT-LNK campaigns — leaned on the LNK-stego initial-access pattern: a single .lnk file that contains a benign decoy (often a Korean .hwp/.docx/.pdf) plus an embedded shellcode/PE blob, which the LNK extracts via cmd.exe + findstr/certutil/PowerShell into AppData an
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
